desc:
Three old and squat oaks are camped up here on this slight rise in the plains,
offering some, though scant, protection to various kinds of birdlife. A slight
depression is situated some distance north from here, offering another sanctuary
from the ear-piercing winds that often blow across this open landscape.
name: Slight Rise

desc:
From the northern end of the horizon, all the way as far south as the eye can
see, a looming shadow rises up from the ground east of here. In all probability,
it could only be a ridge or escarpment of some sort. Slightly lower than the
immediately surrounding plains, this spot gives shelter to a few lonely oak
saplings. Not higher than eight or nine feet, the trees' future shape is already
foreshadowed by their current appearance, as the yearly autumn storms would most
certainly take away anything taller.
name: Plains

desc:
The looming cliff-wall north-east of here seemingly reaches out towards you,
poking a jagged pinnacle-crowned fist-like formation in the general direction of
the mighty Anduin river. Apart from this impressive geographical feature, the
plains that surround the escarpment provide a comparatively lush backdrop to the
stern, sterile ruggedness of the escarpment.
name: Curving Escarpment

desc:
Turning a sharp corner, an escarpment the height of a very large horse blocks
the way north and east. There has been considerable erosion of the cliff face;
thousands and thousands of reddish rock shards lie scattered on the ground,
interspersed with though green-greyish grass and waist-high juniper shrubs.
name: Bend in the Escarpment

desc:
A curious geological formation, this natural escarpment divides the plain into a
lower and a higher part. Northeast of here, a slightly curved, ramp-like path
leads up onto the higher section. Northwards, a reddish rock wall can be seen
rising out of the plain for miles and miles. Looking in a westerly direction,
one sees a fairly lush plain, interspersed with clumps of trees. Far in the
distance, a misty haze above the horizon reveals the location of the mighty
river Anduin.
name: At a Natural Ramp

desc:
The common juniper that grows here has the ability to take on a number of
interesting and bizarre shapes. Here, where the wind and the other elements can
torture the plants throughout their lives, the shrubs opt to stay low and close
to the ground; a fact that makes travel here somewhat difficult, whether mounted
or not.
name: Juniper Plains

desc:
These lush and green plains are the domain of various kinds of wildlife - a fact
that is made evident through various tell-tale signs: here and there, tracks of
mammals of various kinds can be discerned, particularly in the muddy patches
that lie interspersed among the spongy tufts of hardy plains-grasses.
name: Juniper Plains

desc:
Untouched by any kind of civilisation, this plain obviously harbours a varied
flora and fauna. The lush, evergreen grasses that grow here are probably the
staple food for a number of herbivores, evident since the ground bears the marks
of quite intense grazing.
name: Plains

desc:
Both rugged and desolate, this dusty plain offers little incentive for any
vegetation to grow. Only some tough, grey-green grass manages to survive here,
thriving in coloured patches - in stark contrast to the reddish soil that makes
out the ground on this part of the plains. To the west, a steep slope leads down
towards far more fertile plains.
name: Dusty Plain

desc:
At least one can be certain that these lonesome plains are not totally devoid of
life, as many creatures evidently use this spot as a watering spot. This is
quite incontestable, because tracks and droppings of many kinds of animals are
abundant. The lure of the location must be a slight depression in the reddish
plain, a basin-like pocket that serves as a reservoir of life-giving water,
something to be enjoyed by all kinds of local wildlife.
name: A Water Hole

desc:
Most decidedly a great vantage point, this massive red sandstone boulder rises
some fifty feet from the lower plains. The verdant plains gently roll westwards
towards a faraway river. Access to this place is only granted from the air, or
through a severely difficult climb down unto the higher plain that lies on top
of the escarpment. Six consecutively smaller rocks have been piled upon each
other at the highest point as some kind of marker.
name: Top of a Huge Boulder

desc:
Only a few shrubs grow in the field here, the rest is consumed with long blades
of green grass. The grass has a variety of texture to it: some of the blades
have a refined texture, whereas others are very coarse. There are a few weeds
visible in the field as well, though they are not very tall in length, and the
grass hides the majority of them. However, along the northern edge of the field
the soil is exposed, giving off a red hue.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
A few shrubs are growing in the field, spread out wide apart from one another.
There are many leaves on each branch of the shrubs, oval in shape, with a dark
green colour to each. There are several weeds growing throughout the field, very
tall and of different shades of green. The grass is a mixture of green and brown
colour, with varying length. There is a slight incline upon the earth as the
field continues to the north.
name: Mixed Field

desc:
Shorter, fine grass grows among the very long, green blades of the field, making
it very thick in appearance. Tufts of brown grass are visible here and there,
stunted in their growth. Various types of weeds have mixed themselves into the
field among the grass here as well.
name: Mixed Field

desc:
Many weeds are scattered in the field here. The most common weed of the field
has wide, arrow shaped leaves and stands quite tall. The dark, green leaf of
this weed pokes out from everywhere, unlike some other weeds that blend into the
green grass. The grass has grown tall, each blade appearing to have a soft green
texture.
name: Field of Weeds

desc:
The field consists of lengthy grasses of different textures and colours. Some of
the grass has a rough, crisp looking texture to it, with a light tone of brown,
whereas some of the longest blades are green with a soft texture. A few weeds
attempting to blend in among the grass stand tall with prickly, large leaves.
Each leaf on the weeds appears to have a waxy finish to them.
name: Field of Grass

desc:
There is a large variety of grass growing in the field here. Some of the grass
is a soft shade of brown, other grass is fully green in colour. A few stems of
grain have also grown along the western side of the field, blending in with the
long blades of grass. There are also a few shrubs, low in height, which have
grown rather wide. Each shrub is thickly covered with leaves of an oval shape
upon their branches.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
Blended within the long blades of wild grass on the western side of the field
are a few stems of brown grain. The rest of the field is covered very thickly by
long blades of grass with different textures and colours to the blades. A few
weeds grow among the blades of grass, with diamond shaped leaves extending from
the stems. The weeds are a dark green, however, the stems of these weeds are a
much lighter green. A single shrub of a dark green colour grows within the field
as well.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
There are many blades of grass of several different colours growing wild here.
Many weeds grow exceedingly tall on the eastern edge of the field, where the
grass becomes very thick. A few dry shrubs are scattered around the field as
well. The leaves on the shrubs are small and oval in shape.
name: Rough Field

desc:
A large portion of the field here emits an essence of golden-brown. Almost every
blade is slender, tall in length and has a very refined texture to it. However,
at the northern end of the field, hints of green appear, blended in among the
rest of the golden-brown blades of grass. The difference in length of the green
blades is very noticeable, in comparison to those which are brown. The green
grass in the northern portion of the field is very short, whereas the
golden-brown seems to tower over the rest.
name: Golden-brown Field

desc:
Slender, tall blades of grass, all golden-brown in colour, grow in abundance
here in the field. On the south edge of the field the grass begins to diminish
until only bare soil remains. Even the bare soil is of a golden-brown hue. Some
stones are lying upon the earth where the grass has ceased to grow. The stones
are not very large and have a rough looking texture. There is a slight
depression throughout the centre of the field however, in a straight line, which
looks as though something rolled through in a north-south direction.
name: Golden-brown Field

desc:
Scattered around the plain are several large pits in the ground. Near some of
these pits are large rocks that appear as though somehow they were lifted out
from their deep bed of soil, for dirt and mud remain around the base of each
rock. Several of the pits have an odd shape to them, whereas a few of the pits
are extremely circular in shape.
name: Pitted Plain

desc:
The plain presents a desolate appearance. Rough grass of mixed green and brown
colours covers the ground in the middle of the plain. The soil is very dry and
light brown in colour. Eastward, across the plain, there are many rocks of
various sizes. There is a mixture of colour to the rocks, varying in shades of
brown, grey, and black.
name: Mixed Plain

desc:
There is not much to observe in the plain here, as the land appears very sterile
and dry. A few weeds have grown out from beneath the rough ground, along with
small patches of grass that looks as though each blade has been shredded. The
entire area gives off a brown essence, for even the few patches of grass are
parched due to the lack of shade.
name: Sterile Plain

desc:
The soil in the plain is not only bare, but very dry. Only a few slender weeds
have grown, although even these are not very tall in height. Large clumps of dry
soil lie upon the ground, easy to be mistaken for a large stone from a distance.
Along the western side of the plain, a few blades of coarse grass are visible.
name: In a Plain

desc:
Within this den are earthen walls made of natural clay. The den is not
necessarily large but has a rather decent amount of space inside. The clay walls
are all different shades of brown, some are even red, and have a clumpy, natural
look. The den overall has a circular shape to it, with the base having a very
dark colour of brown. Debris is scattered upon the ground of the den, along with
long, dried up blades of grass.
name: Earthy Den

desc:
The walls forming the tunnel are of natural materials. Consisting of a lot of
packed mud, roots, and other plant-like materials, they appear to hold quite
well. A lot of moisture has built up in the tunnel making it very humid. The
space here is very narrow, and becomes even more so near far end, making it
difficult to pass through.
name: Muggy Tunnel

desc:
The narrow, muggy tunnel opens widely into a rather large, old dwelling place of
some sort. The underground dwelling at one time must have been the home to a
small being, as much of the remaining debris appears to once have been made for
a creature of much smaller stature if compared to Men. Though there is a large
portion of stone that was used as a ceiling, it has now been collapsed for what
appears to be a long time. Even with this fallen stone, there is still a
sufficient amount of space within the enclosed tunnel. Between the fallen stones
near the southern corners hang many webs, some weaved in unique patterns.
name: Collapsed End

desc:
This underground chamber's walls were made of natural stones and pieces of rock.
The stones and pieces of rock were firmly placed into the earthen walls with
some kind of black material in between the stones to seal them. The stones were
placed tightly together, even though each is oddly shaped. The floor of the
chamber was laid with several pieces of flat, black slate. Between each piece of
slate there are cracks that were left unfilled with sealant; now water has built
up in between them from all the moisture in the earth. On the eastern wall,
there are two torches braced between stones by small metal brackets. Below the
torches is a scarlet coloured canvas concealing the wall. However, on the
northern wall a lever of golden colour is visible.
name: Underground Chamber

desc:
Within this chamber there are four slender pillars, which are providing support
for keeping a black sheet of slate, used as the ceiling, in its proper place.
The flooring is of the same slate that was used for the ceiling, though there
are many carved grooves upon the surface. The four walls were made of many
natural stones pressed tightly together into the earth, with a black sealant to
fill the tiny spaces between them. In the centre of the chamber, there is a
large, black orb.
name: Inner Chamber

desc:
The library has four walls made of black slate. Two of these walls have a few
shelves built within them, with a golden-coloured lining at the base of each
one. Upon the shelves are several different sculptures and a fleet of model
ships, each looking unique in their fashion and design. The south wall contains
a bookshelf and a large map, with a golden frame holding it to the wall. The
north wall has a dark, cherry-coloured door that has a rounded arch design at
the top of it. Above the arched door, a golden banner was placed on display. The
floor of the library is lined with carpet, matching the colour of the banner
above the arched door. A large candle is set in a brass stand between the
bookcase and the framed map upon the wall, giving off a soft glow of light to
the library.
name: Library

desc:
A wide, round area opens up inside the rock. The floor is smooth, level and
covered by dust. In the eastern wall a niche has been carved out in the rock,
and in the middle of this cavern some flat stones have been gathered into a
circle, offering a comfortable-looking place to sit down and rest around a camp
fire. In the northern wall two pillars have been carved around the steps of a
staircase leading up. The air is still and warm.
name: Underground Camp Site

desc:
Cracks and fissures on the rocky wall form natural, dust-covered steps making it
possible to climb between the cavern below and a narrow opening in the ceiling.
Based on the wisps of fresh air that seem to come from the passageway above,
there is probably an exit out somewhere nearby.
name: Natural Staircase

desc:
This narrow ledge opens a bit above the plain, dominating a hollow in the
grasslands below. A little opening in the hillside leads to a mossy stairway
leading downwards. This shelf is wide enough to be used as a place to rest under
the vastness of the sky.
name: Rock Shelf

desc:
The grass here grows far thicker than almost any other nearby region. Some
cattails have also grown up towards the northern part, adding variety to the
mostly bare scenery.
name: Lush Plains

desc:
In contrast to the rest of the plains, this area has some plant-life other than
grasses. A few hardy shrubs sprout from the ground here, providing a slight
change of scenery from the seemingly endless plains about them. Nestled among
the shrubs is a pool of water with a rather muddy bank. Pawprints in the mud
suggest that this is a frequent spot for the denizens of these lands to stop and
take a drink.
name: At a Waterhole

desc:
The tree above has rotten from inside. Its roots must have been affected for a
much longer time, as the remains that are still visible all around are deeply
black and covered with dry, now also dead fungi. It also seems as if the roots
and all the soil around had been saturated with some sort of black slime that
also has dried by now. The area feels cold and thick mist fills the air that
smells foul of rot and decay.
name: Below the Dead Tree

desc:
The ground looks very unsteady, sand and dirt being ready to shift upon any
pressure laid upon the ground. Parts of the land have already caved in, giving
way to dangerous sinkholes. Especially to the west the ground looks so difficult
that it prevents travelling into that direction.
name: Uneven Plains

desc:
Flat land stretches out in all directions, studded with occasional skinny trees.
The land is very green, tall grass covers most of the plains rippling with every
breeze that passes. The dark shadow of Mirkwood can be seen far to the east.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The wagon would allow a comfortable amount of space from within, providing that
it was in an upright position. However, left in its current position, tilted
over upon its side, the height of it while standing is very short. Judging from
the appearance of the wagon, one would think that it has lain here for many
years. The planks that form the walls look like they were made of some kind of
mahogany. There are several racks that have been attached to the left side of
the wagon wall, placed along each side of the door to the wagon.
name: Inside of a Wagon

desc:
The intricacy of this entanglement of brush creates an almost mystifying network
of snarled passages, which dart to and fro in the vegetation. The paths have
probably been created by wildlife inhabitants of this greenery, although none
can be seen and no other sounds than a low rustling of grass can be heard.
Reaching far above the tall brush blocks the view to the surrounding areas.
name: Entangled Bushes

desc:
The intricacy of this entanglement of brush creates an almost mystifying network
of snarled passages, which dart to and fro in the vegetation. The paths have
probably been created by wildlife inhabitants of this greenery, although none
can be seen and no other sounds than a low rustling of grass can be heard.
Reaching far above the tall brush blocks the view to the surrounding areas.
name: Entangled Bushes
